metadata.title: Important Days and Events Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Where was the 3rd Session of the Council of the International Organization for Marine Aids to Navigation (IALA) inaugurated?
questions.explanation: The 3rd Session of the Council of the International Organization for Marine Aids to Navigation (IALA) was recently inaugurated virtually in Mumbai by the Union Minister for Ports, Shipping and Waterways. Its motto is “Successful Voyages, Sustainable Planet.” It aims to improve and standardise aids to navigation worldwide, promote safe and efficient vessel movement, support new technological developments and strengthen international cooperation.

questions.question: UNICEF Foundation Day is observed on which day every year?
questions.explanation: UNICEF Foundation Day is observed on 11th December every year, 2025 marking the 79th anniversary of the organisation. UNICEF stands for United Nations International Children’s Emergency Fund, renamed in 1953 as United Nations Children’s Fund. It was established in 1946 by the United Nations. UNICEF works on humanitarian grounds to provide emergency assistance and support to children from underprivileged communities. UNICEF has contributed significantly to reducing child mortality, improving education, and supporting vulnerable communities worldwide.

questions.question: Where was the Bhartiya Bhasha Utsav 2025 organized?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Education organised Bhartiya Bhasha Utsav 2025 at National Bal Bhawan, New Delhi on the birth anniversary of Mahakavi Subramania Bharati, with the theme “Many Languages, One Emotion”. The Ministry of Tribal Affairs participated, showcasing tribal language publications from Tribal Research Institutes (TRIs) of Jharkhand, Odisha, and Gujarat, including dictionaries, primers, storybooks, and research documents. Ministry of Tribal Affairs demonstrated Adi Vaani, India’s first AI-powered tribal language translator, developed in collaboration with IIT Delhi, supporting Santali, Kui, Bhili, Mundari, Gondi, and Garo. Bhartiya Bhasha Utsav 2025 highlighted India’s linguistic diversity and the cultural significance of tribal languages, promoting multilingual heritage and inclusion.

questions.question: International Universal Health Coverage (UHC) Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: International Universal Health Coverage (UHC) Day is observed every year on 12 December. It is a UN-designated day, marking the 2012 UN resolution where all member states endorsed UHC as a global development priority. The day urges campaigners, health defenders, and leaders to highlight the struggles of millions still lacking access to healthcare, advocate for stronger health investments, and remind the world of the importance of achieving Universal Health Coverage for all.

questions.question: Which department launched Good Governance Week 2025?

options.label: B
options.text: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade

options.label: D
options.text: Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances

answer.label: D
answer.text: Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances

questions.explanation: The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) launched Good Governance Week 2025 from December 19 to 25, with guidelines for the nationwide ‘Prashasan Gaon Ki Ore’ campaign released by Secretary Rachna Shah. The initiative aims to strengthen citizen-centric governance with a focus on grassroots delivery of public services and grievance redressal. Good Governance Week is observed annually around the birth anniversary of former Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee (December 25) and has evolved into an action-oriented administrative campaign. The ‘Prashasan Gaon Ki Ore’ initiative places district administrations at the forefront of grievance redressal and public service delivery.

questions.question: Which country has been designated as the Guest of Honour at New Delhi World Book Fair (NDWBF) 2026?
questions.explanation: The New Delhi World Book Fair (NDWBF) 2026, the world’s largest book fair, has begun at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi and will continue until January 18, 2026. Entry is free for the first time to promote a strong reading culture across India. It is the 53rd edition, organised by the National Book Trust (NBT) under the Ministry of Education, Government of India. The fair features participation from over 35 countries, more than 1,000 publishers, 3,000+ stalls, 600 events, and over 1,000 speakers. The theme is “Indian Military History: Valour & Wisdom @ 75”, with Qatar as Guest of Honour and Spain as Focus Country.

questions.question: Where was the world’s leading trade fair for security, safety and fire protection, Intersec 2026, held?
questions.explanation: The 27th edition of Intersec, the world’s leading trade fair for security, safety, and fire protection, held at the Dubai World Trade Centre. Intersec 2026 was the largest edition so far, covering 65,000 square metres, with 1,200+ exhibitors from over 60 countries and more than 50,000 visitors. The exhibition showcased innovations across five core sectors, namely homeland security and policing, cybersecurity, commercial and perimeter security, fire and rescue, and health and safety.

questions.question: Which city is the host of 2nd Global Buddhist Summit 2026?
questions.explanation: Recently, the International Buddhist Confederation (IBC) with the Ministry of Culture will organise the 2nd Global Buddhist Summit on 24–25 January 2026 at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi. The Summit is held under the theme “Collective Wisdom, United Voice, and Mutual Coexistence”. It highlights the relevance of Buddha Dhamma values like compassion, wisdom, harmony, and coexistence. The event builds on the first Summit held in 2023. About 200 global delegates, including Supreme Patriarchs, monks, scholars, and leaders, will participate.

questions.question: The International Holocaust Remembrance Day is observed on which day every year?
questions.explanation: The International Holocaust Remembrance Day is observed annually on January 27 across the world. It is observed to honor Jews and others massacred during the Holocaust. It marks the liberation of Auschwitz-Birkenau concentration camp on January 27, 1945, ending World War II. The day serves as a reminder of Holocaust atrocities and promotes the fight against hatred, intolerance, and discrimination. The United Nations has designated the theme for 2026 as “Holocaust Remembrance for Dignity and Human Rights”.

questions.question: Shakti Samvaad 2026 is organized by which organization?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Women and Child Development

questions.explanation: The National Commission for Women (NCW) organized ‘Shakti Samvaad’, an interactive and capacity-building meeting with State Women’s Commissions (SWCs) at Bharat Mandapam, New Delhi on January 29–30, 2026. The two-day event aimed to strengthen institutional coordination, share best practices, and build capacities of SWCs, marking the third national-level Shakti Samvaad after Ayodhya and Mumbai editions.
